description: Approved pre-existing tsc --noEmit errors. Regenerate with `npm run typecheck:baseline -- --update`. New signatures fail the gate.

errors.message: Object literal may only specify known properties, and 'subject' does not exist in type 'ParamsReducerFn<RouterCore<Route<Register, any, "/", "/", string, "__root__", undefined, {}, { queryClient: QueryClient; }, AnyContext, AnyContext, ... 6 more ..., undefined>, "never", false, RouterHistory, Record<...>>, "PATH", string, "/tests/$subject/">'.

Type '"/tests/$subject/"' is not assignable to type '"." | ".." | "/privacy-policy" | "/" | "/about" | "/certificate" | "/contact" | "/current-affairs-test" | "/daily-mission" | "/disclaimer" | "/login" | "/mock-test" | "/model-paper-test" | ... 50 more ... | "/tests/$subject"'. Did you mean '"/tests/$subject"'?
Property 'external' does not exist on type 'PrepareLinkTarget'. Property 'external' does not exist on type '{ to: "/test"; search: { language: "english" | "hindi"; }; }'.
Property 'external' does not exist on type 'PrepareLinkTarget'. Property 'external' does not exist on type '{ to: "/test"; search: { language: "english" | "hindi"; }; }'.
Property 'length' does not exist on type 'never'.
This comparison appears to be unintentional because the types 'true | undefined' and 'false' have no overlap.
